title: Money Foundations
description: Understand income, expenses, budgeting, and why financial literacy is the most important life skill you never learned in school.
nceaStandardTitle: Demonstrate understanding of an organisation's financial decision-making
rationale: Aligned with NCEA Level 1 Commerce AS92028. Students demonstrate understanding of financial decision-making using budgets and cost-benefit analysis as financial tools. Pūtake is embedded in all scenarios to meet the mandatory AS92028 requirement.
learningObjectives: Define income, expenses, surplus and deficit in a NZ context
Apply the 50/30/20 budgeting rule to real NZ scenarios
Use a budget as a financial tool to support a decision
Identify and explain stakeholder perspectives in financial decisions
Discuss pūtake in the context of an organisation's financial decision-making
Calculate gross and net pay using NZ tax rates
Evaluate the financial viability of personal and organisational decisions
Complete an NCEA-style financial decision-making assessment task
assessmentTask: Students choose a real or fictional NZ organisation facing a financial need, issue or opportunity. They describe three options using a financial tool (budget or cost-benefit analysis), recommend a decision supported by financial data, identify stakeholder perspectives, discuss pūtake, and analyse consequences at Achieved, Merit, and Excellence levels.
